info.title: Amperity Real-time API
info.description:
## Overview

The Amperity Real-time API provides programmatic access to real-time customer profiles and segment membership in your Amperity tenant. Use Real-time API endpoints to look up live customer profiles by identity key, retrieve profile attributes, and query real-time segment membership for use in personalization, activation, and engagement workflows.

## Versioning

The Real-time API is currently in an **unstable** release. Unstable versions contain features that are still in progress and may not be backward compatible.

Unstable versions do not guarantee customer support, notification of changes or breaking changes, or availability.

## Authentication

All requests made to Real-time API endpoints must be authenticated using an API key that has the **Real-time API data access** policy attached.

Authenticate by including the following header in each request:

```
-H "Authorization: Bearer ${access-token}"
```

After the token passes validation, the request will look up any access policies attached to the API key, and then determine whether the requested operation is permitted.

> **Important:** A user must be assigned the **Allow API key administration** policy before they can manage API keys and access tokens required by Amperity APIs.

### API keys

Amperity API keys are synthetic identities that are bound to your tenant and enable programmatic access to Amperity. Each API key has a unique internal secret that is signed into the claims of all access tokens issued for that API key.

API keys are tenant-specific and are not pulled to a sandbox or promoted from a sandbox to production. API keys must be created directly in a sandbox.

### Access tokens

Access to the Real-time API requires using [JSON Web Token (JWT)](https://jwt.io/) access tokens that are signed by Amperity-managed API keys. Because a JWT access token automatically expires, tokens should be refreshed on a regular basis.

Access tokens are managed from the **Settings** page in Amperity. Open **Settings**, select the **Security** tab, find the API key, and select **Get token** from the actions menu.

> **Important:** You are the only person who has access to the newly generated access token. Amperity does not save the access token. Store it in a safe place.

## Base URL

All requests made to Real-time API endpoints should be directed to the base URL.

### Amazon AWS

Tenants hosted in Amazon AWS use the following base URL:

```
https://app.amperity.com/prof
```

Use the `Amperity-Tenant` header to provide the tenant ID. You can find the tenant ID from the Amperity user interface: open **Settings**, select the **Security** tab, and under **API keys** select **Copy tenant ID** from the menu for an API key.

### Microsoft Azure

Tenants hosted in Microsoft Azure use the following base URL:

```
https://<tenant-id>.amperity.com/prof
```

The tenant ID must be in the base URL *and* in the `Amperity-Tenant` header.

### Australia

Tenants hosted in Australia use the following default base URL:

```
https://app-aws-apse2.amperity.com/prof
```

## Requests

Requests made to Real-time API endpoints require the following headers:

- **Authorization** Required. The bearer authentication header. Use the access token for your tenant's API key.
- **Amperity-Tenant** Required. The ID for the tenant to which the request is sent.

**Example**

```
curl -request GET \
     -url "https://app.amperity.com/prof/profiles/{collection-id}/lookup?email=user@example.com" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer ${access-token}" \
     -H "Amperity-Tenant: <tenant-id>"
```

## Responses

Real-time API endpoints use conventional HTTP response status codes to indicate success or failure.

### 2xx status codes

| Status code | Description |
|---|---|
| **200 OK** | A request completed successfully. |

### 4xx status codes

| Status code | Description |
|---|---|
| **400 Bad Request** | A request did not contain a required parameter or has an invalid parameter. |
| **401 Authentication Required** | A request contains invalid authentication or an expired token. |
| **403 Not Authorized** | A request contains valid authentication, but does not have permission to perform the requested action. |
| **404 Not Found** | A request was made to a resource that does not exist. |
| **429 Too Many Requests** | The rate limit has been exceeded. |

### 5xx status codes

| Status code | Description |
|---|---|
| **500 Server Error** | An internal error occurred. |

## Pagination

Amperity uses cursor-based pagination to return pages of data for large lists. A cursor acts like a pointer and refers to a particular point in the data, marking the boundary between pages. A paginated endpoint returns responses with a list of results and a **next_token** parameter when another page is available. You have reached the last page when **next_token** is not returned.

### Pagination in requests

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| **limit** | The maximum number of records to include in a single page of results. |
| **next_token** | An opaque token used to paginate results. Omit to return the first page. Use the value returned in a prior response to view the next page. Cannot be **NULL**. |

### Pagination in responses

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| **data** | A JSON array of values for the current page of results. |
| **next_token** | The cursor value to use in a subsequent request to return the next page. When empty, the last page has been returned. |
| **total** | The total count of all results. |

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: Look up real-time customer profiles from a profile collection.

tags.name: Segments
tags.description: Query real-time segment membership for profiles.

get.description:
Use the **GET /profiles/{collection-id}/lookup** endpoint to look up a customer profile from a collection using an identity key.

Provide exactly one identity key as a query parameter. The key name must match a linking key configured for the collection. If the key matches a profile, the full profile is returned. If no match is found, a **404** response is returned.

Optionally include `enrich=true` to return enriched profile data.

### Example

```
curl -request GET \
     -url "https://app.amperity.com/prof/profiles/{collection-id}/lookup?email=user@example.com" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer ${access-token}" \
     -H "Amperity-Tenant: <tenant-id>"
```

parameters.name: enrich
parameters.description: Set to `true` to return enriched profile data.

parameters.name: <linking-key>
parameters.description:
The identity linking key and value to use for the lookup. Exactly one linking key must be provided. Replace `<linking-key>` with the name of a configured identity key for this collection.

For example: `?email=user@example.com` or `?loyalty_id=123456789`.

200.description: OK — returns the matching customer profile.

400.description: Bad Request — exactly one linking key must be specified.

example.message: Exactly one lookup key must be specified

404.description: Not Found — no profile matched the specified linking key.

get.description:
Use the **GET /profiles/{collection-id}/stats** endpoint to return statistics for a profile collection.

Returns aggregate information about the collection, including profile counts and last-updated timestamps.

### Example

```
curl -request GET \
     -url "https://app.amperity.com/prof/profiles/{collection-id}/stats" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer ${access-token}" \
     -H "Amperity-Tenant: <tenant-id>"
```

200.description: OK — returns statistics for the specified profile collection.

get.description:
Use the **GET /profiles/{collection-id}/{profile-id}** endpoint to return a specific customer profile from a collection.

The `collection-id` identifies the profile collection. The `profile-id` identifies the individual customer profile within that collection.

Optionally include `enrich=true` to return enriched profile data.

### Example

```
curl -request GET \
     -url "https://app.amperity.com/prof/profiles/{collection-id}/{profile-id}" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer ${access-token}" \
     -H "Amperity-Tenant: <tenant-id>"
```

parameters.name: enrich
parameters.description: Set to `true` to return enriched profile data.

200.description: OK — returns the specified customer profile.

404.description: Not Found — no profile with the specified ID exists in the collection.

example.message: No profile in collection col-abc123 with id prf-xyz789

get.description:
Use the **GET /profiles/{collection-id}/{profile-id}/segments** endpoint to return a paginated list of real-time segments that the specified profile is a member of.

Each result includes the segment ID and segment name.

### Example

```
curl -request GET \
     -url "https://app.amperity.com/prof/profiles/{collection-id}/{profile-id}/segments" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer ${access-token}" \
     -H "Amperity-Tenant: <tenant-id>"
```

200.description: OK — returns a paginated list of segments the profile belongs to.

get.description:
Use the **GET /segments/{segment-id}/profiles** endpoint to return a paginated list of profiles that are members of the specified real-time segment.

Each result includes the profile ID.

### Example

```
curl -request GET \
     -url "https://app.amperity.com/prof/segments/{segment-id}/profiles" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer ${access-token}" \
     -H "Amperity-Tenant: <tenant-id>"
```

200.description: OK — returns a paginated list of profiles that are members of the segment.

BearerAuth.description: Authenticate using a Bearer token generated from an Amperity API key with the Real-time API data access policy. Include the token in the Authorization header of every request.

AmperityTenant.name: Amperity-Tenant
AmperityTenant.description: The unique identifier for the tenant.

CollectionId.name: collection-id
CollectionId.description: The unique identifier for the profile collection.

ProfileId.name: profile-id
ProfileId.description: The unique identifier for the customer profile.

SegmentId.name: segment-id
SegmentId.description: The unique identifier for the real-time segment.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: The maximum number of records to include in a single page of results.

NextToken.name: next_token
NextToken.description:
An opaque token that is used to paginate results. Omit the `next_token` property to return the first page. Use the cursor value for `next_token` that was returned in a response to view the next page of results.

> **Important:** The value for `next_token` cannot be null.

BadRequest.description: Bad Request — the request was malformed or contained invalid parameters.

Unauthorized.description: Authentication Required — no valid Bearer token was provided or the token has expired.

Forbidden.description: Not Authorized — the API key does not have permission for this operation.

NotFound.description: Not Found — the requested resource does not exist.

example.message: The requested entity does not exist.

TooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests — the rate limit has been exceeded.

InternalError.description: Internal Server Error — an unexpected error occurred.

ErrorResponse.description: An error response returned by the API.

message.description: A human-readable summary of the error.
message.example: A human-readable summary of the error.

request_id.description: A unique identifier for the request.

ProfileAttributes.description: A free-form object containing the attribute fields for a customer profile. The fields present in this object depend on the attributes configured for the collection.

Profile.description: A customer profile from a real-time profile collection.

collection_id.description: The unique identifier for the profile collection.

profile_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer profile.

created_at.description: The timestamp at which the profile was created.

updated_at.description: The timestamp at which the profile was last updated.

ticked_at.description: The timestamp at which the profile was last processed by the identity resolution pipeline.

links.description: Identity links associated with this profile.

CollectionStats.description: Aggregate statistics for a profile collection.

collection_id.description: The unique identifier for the profile collection.

profile_count.description: The total number of profiles in the collection.

updated_at.description: The timestamp at which the collection statistics were last updated.

SegmentMembership.description: A real-time segment that a profile is a member of.

segment_id.description: The unique identifier for the real-time segment.

segment_name.description: The display name of the real-time segment.

SegmentMembershipListResponse.description: A paginated list of real-time segments a profile belongs to.

data.description: A JSON array of segment membership objects for the current page of results.

next_token.description: The cursor value to use in a subsequent request to return the next page of results. When empty, the last page has been returned.

total.description: The total number of segments the profile belongs to.

SegmentProfile.description: A profile that is a member of a real-time segment.

profile_id.description: The unique identifier for the customer profile.

SegmentProfileListResponse.description: A paginated list of profiles that are members of a real-time segment.

data.description: A JSON array of profile objects for the current page of results.

next_token.description: The cursor value to use in a subsequent request to return the next page of results. When empty, the last page has been returned.

total.description: The total number of profiles in the segment.
